sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Lecce are struggling, winless in their last three matches with two losses and a draw, conceding 6 goals while scoring only 1. Parma have shown mixed results, with a recent win against Fiorentina but a loss to Inter and a draw against Sassuolo, managing just 2 goals in their last 3 games. Both teams are in the lower half of the table, with Parma slightly ahead in 15th (18 points) and Lecce in 16th (17 points).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Lecce have had the upper hand, winning both matches (1-0 and 3-1) against Parma. This gives Lecce a psychological edge, having scored 4 goals to Parma's 1 in these fixtures, with no losses in their last three head-to-head games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have struggled offensively, each scoring just 12 goals this season, ranking 19th in Serie A for goals scored. Lecce have conceded more (25 vs Parma's 21), but they excel in tackles (18.6 per game, 2nd in the league) and clearances (28.1 per game, 3rd). Parma’s recent games suggest a low-scoring trend with under 2.5 goals in all of their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Lecce, Lameck Banda (2 goals, 1 assist) and Riccardo Sottil (1 goal, 1 assist) are key attacking threats despite missing some players like Medon Berisha. Parma’s Mateo Pellegrino stands out with 5 goals and 1 assist, overperforming his xG, making him a potential game-changer.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 44% chance for a draw, combined with both teams’ low-scoring tendencies and Lecce’s recent head-to-head dominance, a tight, low-scoring game is likely. Lecce might have a slight edge at home, but Parma’s defensive resilience could force a stalemate.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Lecce and Parma at Via del Mare saw Lecce take an early lead with a goal in the opening minute by Nikola Štulić. Lecce have managed to hold onto their slender 1-0 advantage, showing a slightly more clinical edge in front of goal despite having less possession of the ball. Parma, on the other hand, dominated the ball but failed to create any significant scoring opportunities, struggling to break through Lecce's defensive structure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Lecce have been effective in their attacking third, creating a notable chance and converting it early on. Their entries into the final third and penalty area have been more frequent than Parma's, indicating a direct approach in attack. Defensively, they've been solid, winning a decent share of aerial duels and maintaining organization. Parma, despite holding the majority of possession, have lacked the cutting edge in the final third. Their inability to create big chances and lower frequency of penalty area entries highlight a disconnect in their attacking play, though they’ve shown slightly better duel-winning ability on the ground.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Lecce, Nikola Štulić has been the standout performer, not only scoring the decisive goal but also posing a constant threat with his positioning. Lameck Banda has also been influential, contributing with an assist and creating dangerous moments in attack. On Parma's side, players like Adrián Bernabé have tried to orchestrate play from midfield with key passes, while Emanuele Valeri showed some intent with efforts on target. However, Parma’s forward line has struggled to make a meaningful impact against Lecce’s backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The game began with Lecce showing strong intent, quickly establishing momentum with a burst of attacking play right from the kick-off, which led to their early goal. As the half progressed, Parma responded with sustained spells of pressure, particularly in the middle stages, pushing forward in waves but failing to truly test the Lecce defense. Towards the latter part of the half, Lecce regained control with several intense attacking phases, dominating the momentum and looking more likely to add to their tally. Parma had a brief resurgence in the closing minutes, but it wasn't enough to shift the balance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Lecce will need to focus on maintaining their defensive solidity while capitalizing on counter-attacking opportunities in the second half. They’ve shown vulnerability at times with limited possession, which could be exploited if Parma find rhythm. Parma must improve their creativity and penetration in the final third, as their possession has not translated into dangerous chances. Finding ways to break down Lecce’s compact shape and involving their forwards more effectively will be key for them to get back into the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle, with Lecce likely to sit back and protect their lead while looking for quick transitions to catch Parma off-guard. Parma, trailing by a goal, will push for an equalizer, potentially leaving spaces at the back for Lecce to exploit. Given the first-half dynamics, Lecce appear to have the upper hand with their clinical finishing, but Parma’s persistence and possession play could yield results if they sharpen their attack. Expect both teams to make tactical adjustments, with Parma possibly introducing fresh legs to inject energy.
